prestazioni: Replace the original 1.5L Colombo V12 (Type 125) with a fully balanced and blueprinted version featuring forged Mahle pistons (85mm bore, 70mm stroke), Carrillo H-beam connecting rods, and a billet crankshaft from JE Pistons to handle 12.5:1 compression for 180+ hp at 7,500 RPM on 100-octane fuel, paired with a custom camshaft grind from Schrick (280° duration, 12mm lift) for optimized high-RPM breathing

Install a bespoke dual Weber 45DCOE carburetion system with velocity stacks, custom jetting (165 main, 190 air correctors), and a hand-fabricated 3-inch stainless steel exhaust manifold terminating in a straight-pipe setup with a Scuderia Ferrari-inspired megaphone design (no mufflers) for unfiltered V12 acoustics and a 15% reduction in backpressure

Upgrade the original 4-speed non-synchro gearbox with a close-ratio 5-speed dog-ring transmission from Hewland (LD200 model) featuring a Quaife ATB differential, custom billet shift forks, and a lightweight magnesium bellhousing to reduce rotational mass by 18 lbs, paired with a Tilton twin-plate ceramic clutch for 500+ lb-ft torque capacity
estetica: Fabricate a hand-beaten aluminum body replica of the 1947 125 S using 0.8mm 5052-H32 aluminum sheets, riveted in the original Ferrari style, with a custom front splitter and rear diffuser from a modern 458 GT3 aero kit (scaled down 15%) for 30% more downforce at 100 mph, finished in original Rosso Corsa (Ferrari code 300) with hand-painted Scuderia Ferrari shields

Replace the original steel wheels with custom 16x6 (front) and 16x8 (rear) magnesium knock-off wheels from Borrani (model 500M), wrapped in Pirelli P Zero Corsa tires (185/55R16 front, 225/50R16 rear) for period-correct aesthetics with modern grip, paired with a bespoke Wilwood 4-piston caliper brake kit (13-inch rotors) for 1940s-era stopping power

Install a full interior retrim using hand-stitched Connolly leather (code VA0001) in original Tan color, featuring a custom MOMO Prototipo steering wheel (380mm diameter, suede rim) with a quick-release hub, a period-correct Smiths chronometric tachometer (0-8,000 RPM), and a bespoke roll cage from Custom Cages (T45 steel, 2.5-inch diameter) with integrated fire suppression system (Lifeline Zero 360)
